title: sambucus racemosa f. leucocarpa
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. leucocarpa, also known as the white elder or American white elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, which have been attributed to its rich content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. The medicinal properties of Sambucus racemosa f. leucocarpa have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Sambucus racemosa f. leucocarpa include elderberry syrup, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to prevent and treat respiratory infections, as well as to boost the immune system and support overall well-being.
